headlines.title: Special Report: Looking back on the political legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham
headlines.description: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who had held his Senate seat since 2003 and was seen as a war hawk and significant voice on defense and foreign policy, has died at the age of 71 from a “sudden illness.” NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin and Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker report on Graham’s rise in Congress, his relationship with President Donald Trump, and his views on the wars in Iran and between Russia and Ukraine.

headlines.title: Questions about who will replace Sen. Graham as new image of Sen. McConnell released
headlines.description: Tonight lawmakers from both parties are remembering the life and legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), who died at the age of 71. There are new questions tonight about who will replace him in South Carolina, and growing scrutiny about fellow Republican Mitch McConnell’s health after a newly released statement and photo of the 84-year-old GOP senator. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

headlines.title: Graham makes final remarks in Ukraine a day before his death
headlines.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and made public remarks during a trip to Kyiv just one day before his death. Graham said he was focused on advancing legislation aimed at imposing sanctions on Russian oil.

headlines.title: Sam Neill, actor known for ‘Jurassic Park,’ dies at 78
headlines.description: Sam Neill, the New Zealand actor best known for his role in the “Jurassic Park” movies, has died, his family said Monday.

headlines.title: Mitch McConnell says he suffered fall, was unconscious in first statement since hospitalization
headlines.description: McConnell, R-Ky., said he would return to the Senate but didn’t give a date.

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes and conflicting claims about the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: U.S. Central Command announced a new wave of strikes against Iran aimed at stopping the attack of commercial ships traveling through the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes come as Iran claims the Strait is closed, something U.S. officials dispute. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest from the Middle East.

headlines.title: Ramiro Córdova: “Francia es muy favorita sobre España” | El Pelotazo
headlines.description: El seleccionado francés es el equipo que mejor fútbol ha jugado y ser muy fuerte en todas sus líneas, además que cualquiera de sus jugadores pueden marcar la diferencia.

headlines.title: Roque Santa Cruz: “Paraguay dio un paso importante” | Rumbo a la gran final
headlines.description: El seleccionado paraguayo sorprendió al mundo al eliminar a Alemania y plantarle cara a Francia, demostrando un gran crecimiento con jugadores que militan en las mejores ligas

headlines.title: Dely Valdés: “Faltó el gol de Panamá” | Rumbo a la gran final
headlines.description: El experto analizó el desempeño del seleccionado canalero y aunque reconoció un crecimiento, también sabe que necesitan apoyar el proyecto y no truncarlo.

headlines.title: Antonio Valencia: “Es hora de que Ecuador gane algo” | Rumbo a la gran final
headlines.description: Nuestro experto resaltó la actuación frente a Alemania, pero dejó mucho que desear ante Cabo Verde y ante México, pero confía en que para el próximo mundial serán un equipo más fuerte.

headlines.title: Dunga: “Brasil debe volver a ser Brasil” | Rumbo a la Gran Final
headlines.description: El exfutbolista reconoce que la Canarinha ha perdido la esencia que tantos títulos les dio y su eliminación temprana causó mucha tristeza.

headlines.title: ¿A quién ha favorecido el arbitraje? Martín Vázquez nos dice | Rumbo a la Gran Final
headlines.description: La polémica en las decisiones arbitrales han estado a la orden del día en la presente Copa Mundial, pero un experto nos aclara a quién ha perjudicado y a quién ha favorecido.
